# Chimeraforge

> Personal AI platform — JARVIS-level autonomous AI infrastructure running on local hardware. Site and PyPI brand for the Chimera constitutional AI architecture.

## Naming

- **Chimera** is the constitutional AI architecture itself: debate engine, multi-model consensus, embedding-based safety routing, BFT/Ed25519 provenance, ZK proofs, RLAIF self-improving loop.
- **Chimeraforge** is the public site (https://chimeraforge.vercel.app) and the PyPI package (`pip install chimeraforge`, an LLM deployment optimizer CLI).
- The two names are distinct on purpose. Do not collapse them.
- **quantfit** (`pip install quantfit`) is a separate, standalone CLI — a GPU-aware quantization tool with a built-in safety-tax check. It is NOT part of the Chimera ecosystem; it is an independent tool that productizes the safety-under-quantization research line.

## What This Is

Chimera is a constitutional AI enforcement architecture built by Sahil Kadadekar. It is NOT a chatbot, NOT a streaming tool, and NOT a portfolio site. The platform runs on consumer hardware and handles calendar, inbox, smart home, memory, proactive intelligence, and tool execution through a JARVIS gateway with 7 modules (calendar, inbox, memory, smart home, proactive, tools, store).

## Scale

- 9 repositories across Python, Rust, TypeScript, C#, JavaScript
- 1,348,000+ research measurements across 55 technical reports
- 1 paper accepted at the ICML 2026 Workshop on Hypothesis Testing (workshop, not the main conference)
- 5 papers under peer review (target: top ML venues)
- 268 development episodes (194 Banterpacks + 74 Chimera) — per-commit episode stream now archived; the final episode in each stream is a full retrospective, and ongoing work is documented in the research reports
- PyPI package: `pip install chimeraforge`
- QuantSafe Certifier — live HuggingFace Space: signed release-gate for quantized models (RTSI + Ed25519), built on arXiv:2606.10154

## The Repositories

### 1. Banterpacks (Python, Rust, TypeScript)
Core monorepo. Six interconnected subsystems handling constitutional AI enforcement, multi-model debate, cryptographic provenance, and the JARVIS multi-modal assistant. Includes a 7-crate Rust runtime with Ed25519 provenance, BFT consensus, and zero-knowledge proofs.

### 2. Banterhearts (Python)
ML research platform. Inference API (FastAPI), benchmarking infrastructure, AutoOpt agent (Thompson sampling, multi-armed bandit, SLA enforcement), 37-file evaluation framework. 4 compilation backends (PyTorch Compile, ONNX, JIT, FX), 5 quantization formats. Source of the 1.34M+ measurements across 55 reports.

### 3. Chimeraforge (Python, Rust)
LLM deployment optimizer published on PyPI (v0.5.0). `chimeraforge plan` CLI — model-agnostic 5-gate capacity planner (VRAM, Quality, Safety [opt-in], Latency, Cost) that plans any registry / Ollama / HuggingFace model across 15 GPU profiles. 6 validated predictive models (VRAM R²=0.968, throughput R²=0.859) plus an opt-in safety lookup (TR134 refusal + TR142 RTSI), dual-language harnesses, 450 tests.

### 4. Chimera Multi-Agent (Python)
Muse Protocol — 6-agent content pipeline with ClickHouse analytics. Also the ecosystem observability layer (OpenTelemetry, Datadog, dead-letter queue with 102 tests).

### 5. Chimeradroid (C# / Unity)
Android companion for JARVIS — voice, chat, session handoff, tool approval, mesh networking, offline-first.

### 6. Echo (Python)
Messaging channel adapters — Slack and Discord bridges to JARVIS with session tracking and device key auth.

### 7. JARVIS Console (TypeScript / Next.js)
Web console for JARVIS — chat with streaming, control room dashboard, cognitive agent ELO, tool catalog, workflow management, memory browser.

### 8. Banterblogs / Chimeraforge Site (TypeScript / Next.js)
This site (https://chimeraforge.vercel.app). Auto-generated episodes from git commits across all repos, research archive of 55 technical reports with phase whitepapers + conclusive synthesis docs + extended appendices. Next.js 16 with SSG + ISR.

### 9. Project Wyvern
Embodied autonomy extension of the constitutional architecture. Governed mission-execution plane between Chimera control and PX4/ArduPilot, with a 5-tier authority hierarchy, cryptographic mission replay, and OpenAPI 3.1 mission contract. Phase 0 specs complete; SIM-ONLY MVP on PX4 + ROS 2 + Gazebo in progress.

## Research Program — 1,348,000+ Measurements

Independent ML research with CUDA event timing and controlled safety evaluations. All measurements are reproducible with defined hardware profiles, statistical methodology, and raw data.

### Hardware
- Primary: NVIDIA RTX 4080 Laptop GPU (12GB VRAM), 13th Gen Intel i9
- Extension: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (98GB VRAM) via Google Colab

### Phases
- **Phase 1 — Foundation (TR108–TR116):** Model loading, ONNX conversion, quantization baselines, security analysis.
- **Phase 2 — Benchmarking (TR117–TR122):** Cross-backend inference parity, TensorRT compilation, scaling laws, the compile paradox, the physics of inference.
- **Phase 3 — Optimization (TR123–TR133):** KV-cache tuning, INT8/FP8 quantization, context scaling (512–32K tokens), N-agent scaling, GPU kernel profiling, predictive capacity planner.
- **Phase 4 — Safety Pivot (TR134–TR137):** Alignment under quantization, AWQ/GPTQ safety, backend-driven template divergence, cross-axis safety taxonomy.
- **Phase 5 — Attack Surface (TR138–TR143):** Batch perturbation (TR138 + Study D batch-invariant-kernel ablation), multi-turn jailbreaks, long-context exploitation, cross-architecture fragility, quality-safety divergence, cross-request composition. 306,996+ samples, 18+ models.
- **Phase 6 — Serving-State Safety Certification (TR144–TR149+TR152):** Speculative decoding null (TR144), KV-cache safety null (TR145), mechanistic probing (TR146), portability validation (TR147), multi-judge triangulation (TR148, κ = 0.69, dual-axis methodology), standardized FP16-vs-FP8 KV-cache battery (TR149), serving-state factorial across batch / prefix-caching / temperature (TR152 v2 — 45,000 records, 20,754 matched pairs).
- **Phase 7 — Mitigation Turn (TR155–TR163):** First applied defenses against the failure modes Phases 4–6 detected — RTSI-gated quantization routing (TR163), attention-sink eviction and refusal (TR155), refusal-direction geometry (TR160). Proof-of-mechanism pilots; paper-grade expansion in progress.
- **Phase 8 — Serving-Stack Mechanism Isolation (TR164–TR165):** Serving-stack physics on consumer GPUs. TR164 V1: a sharp N=2 parallel-efficiency breakdown on in-process pytorch_direct, GIL-attributed. TR165 (Python 3.14t nogil ablation): the GIL is a mechanism (~+17.9pp at N=2, 2/6 hangs resolved) but not the sole cause — verdict H2_partial. TR164 V3 (matched A100 80GB cross-backend, vLLM vs SGLang, 189,000 rows): continuous batching holds 0.47–0.62 parallel efficiency at N=32, a workload-conditional vLLM/SGLang routing signal. TR164 V4 (672-cell A100/H100 amortization grid): a one-parameter KV-bandwidth model η(B)=(1+r)/(1+Br) predicts the batching breakdown knee (ρ=0.84, zero fitted params); V5 adds served-SGLang validation + weight-axis evidence closure. The serving-stack-physics paper from this line is in revision for resubmission.
- **Phase 9 — Predictive-Validity Follow-ups (TR166–TR168):** v2 tests of the program's three named methodological screens — RTSIv2 (TR166), JTPv2 (TR167), CRIv2 (TR168): does each calibrated index predict its target on a disjoint hold-out, not just fit in-sample? TR167 lands a structural-degenerate-class finding for JTP on production-quantization substrate. In flight — only TR167 promoted so far.

### Synthesis Documents
Each completed phase (1–6) has a conclusive synthesis (dissertation-style), extended appendices, and an executive decision whitepaper:
- Phase 1 (TR108–TR116): Language, architecture, runtime, and model selection
- Phase 2 (TR117–TR122): Performance, cost, scaling, compiler behavior
- Phase 3 (TR123–TR133): Economics, quantization, context scaling, serving stacks
- Phase 4 (TR134–TR137): Quantization-induced alignment erosion, concurrency invariance, backend template divergence
- Phase 5 (TR138–TR143): Safety attack-surface synthesis, 306,996+ samples, 18+ models, 10+ architecture families; TR138 Study D batch-invariant-kernel addendum
- Phase 6 (TR144–TR149+TR152): Serving-state safety certification — measurement-validity substrate + FP8 KV-cache batteries + serving-state factorial

### Headline Findings
- **Q4_K_M is the universal quantization sweet spot** (-4.1pp accuracy max across 5 models, 30-67% cost savings).
- **Q2_K is universally unacceptable for safety** (100% ASR; banned across 18+ models, 10+ families).
- **Quality metrics are not safety proxies** — safety degrades 13.9× faster than quality at Q3_K_S.
- **Backend choice can move safety by 25pp** (chat template divergence, not the framework).
- **Alignment type does not predict batch fragility** (RLHF/SFT/DPO/distilled all show same flip pattern, p = 0.942).
- **FP8 KV-cache produces no measurable safety degradation** across 24K paired records (TR145, null result).
- **Single-judge safety labels are insufficient** — cross-LLM κ = 0.69 lands in the "triangulate" bucket; safety-specialist judges measure a different axis than general LLMs (TR148, 68K judge rows).
- **Speculative decoding × safety is a clean null** at 16,783 samples (TR144).

## Papers — 1 Accepted, 5 Under Peer Review, 5 In Preparation

### Accepted — ICML 2026 Workshop on Hypothesis Testing
- A Paired Testing Protocol for Batch-Conditioned Refusal Robustness in LLM Serving (TR138; working title "Batch Inference Safety Under Non-Determinism") — accepted 2026-05-22; camera-ready in preparation. arXiv: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27763 (cs.LG). This is a workshop acceptance, not the ICML 2026 main conference.

### Under peer review (target: top ML venues)
1. Compile-Stack Attribution (TR126, TR147)
2. Quality Is Not a Safety Proxy Under Quantization (TR125, TR134, TR142) — arXiv:2606.10154 · live demo: QuantSafe Certifier (https://huggingface.co/spaces/build-small-hackathon/quantsafe-certifier)
3. Many-Shot Jailbreak Under Quantization (TR140)
4. Speculative Decoding Safety — Null Result (TR144)
5. Multi-Turn Jailbreak × Quantization (TR139)

### In Preparation
- Inference Optimization Is Not Safety-Neutral (TR134, TR135, TR136, TR137 synthesis)
- Empirical Capacity Planning for Local LLM Inference (TR123, TR127, TR133)
- Multi-Agent Runtime Architecture (TR112, TR114, TR115)
- KV-Cache Quantization and Safety (TR145)
- Serving-Stack Physics: When Continuous Batching Stops Amortizing (TR164, TR130, TR132) — bandwidth-amortization model; revising for resubmission

## JARVIS Gateway

Multi-modal AI gateway with multi-provider routing (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama). Every chat turn routes through the constitutional router. Tool execution uses a 3-stage propose/approve/execute pipeline with cryptographic provenance on every action. 7 modules: Calendar, Inbox, Memory, Smart Home, Proactive, Tools, Voice.

## Constitutional AI

Embedding-based fast-path router classifies 99% of queries in <10ms via cosine similarity with calibration (isotonic/Platt). Uncertain queries escalate to a multi-model debate engine with heat-based escalation and three consensus algorithms (weighted, ranked-choice, Condorcet). Self-improving via debate-outcome DPO training pairs with statistical drift detection (Welch's t-test, Cohen's d, KS, PSI, ADWIN) and a canary deployment controller with auto-rollback.

## Design System

"Obsidian & Ember" — single hot-copper accent on a near-black background. Cyan accent was dropped in favor of disciplined single-accent palette ("torch in a cave" — restraint everywhere except purposeful highlights).
- Background: hsl(215, 28%, 4%)
- Primary (ember/copper): hsl(16, 90%, 58%) / #ea6533

## PyPI Package

- Package: chimeraforge
- Version: 0.5.0
- Install: `pip install chimeraforge`
- Python: >=3.10
- License: MIT
- CLI: `chimeraforge plan` — model-agnostic 5-gate capacity planner (VRAM, quality, safety [opt-in], latency, cost) for any registry / Ollama / HuggingFace model; plus `suggest` / `catalog` / `measure` (v0.5.0 adds model-agnostic planning; 0.3.0 added the opt-in safety gate)
- URL: https://pypi.org/project/chimeraforge/

## Companion CLI: quantfit (standalone — NOT part of the Chimera ecosystem)

- Package: quantfit
- Version: 0.1.0 (Alpha)
- Install: `pip install quantfit`
- Python: >=3.10
- License: Apache-2.0
- CLI: `quantfit check` (will a model fit the GPU? no download) · `quantfit quantize --method awq|gptq|smoothquant|fp8|rtn|gguf` · `quantfit verify-safety` (did quantization break refusals?) — reports the safety tax as a two-axis vector (refusal-robustness + over-refusal, per zone) judged by the QuantSafe refusal classifier
- URL: https://pypi.org/project/quantfit/
- GitHub: https://github.com/Sahil170595/quantfit
